subject: Science (CBSE)
overall_learning_objectives: Understand what electric current and voltage mean in simple terms
Recognize common electrical appliances and their power usage
Learn basic circuit symbols and how to draw simple circuits
See how series and parallel connections work in daily life
Know why electrical safety is important
Relate electricity concepts to things used at home

lesson_plan.title: What is Electricity?
teaching_strategy: Show pictures of battery, wires, bulb
Simple analogy: water flow in pipe
Class discussion on how lights/fans work
student_activities: List 5 things at home that use electricity
Share one safety rule they know

lesson_plan.title: Circuit Symbols and Basic Circuits
topics_covered: Common symbols: battery, bulb, switch, wire
teaching_strategy: Demonstrate simple circuit with battery & bulb
student_activities: Make a paper circuit diagram with switch & bulb

lesson_plan.title: Series vs Parallel Connections
topics_covered: What happens if one bulb goes off
teaching_strategy: Live demo with 2-3 bulbs in series and parallel
Ask students to predict brightness and behavior
student_activities: Draw both series and parallel circuits

lesson_plan.title: Power of Appliances
topics_covered: Why some appliances use more electricity
teaching_strategy: Show labels on real appliances (or pictures)
Simple explanation: higher watt = more energy used
student_activities: List 5 appliances and their wattage (from home)
Discuss why we switch off unused lights

lesson_plan.title: Heating Effect and Safety
topics_covered: Current produces heat (bulb glows, iron gets hot)
teaching_strategy: Show glowing bulb or heater picture
Discuss fuse wire melting for safety
student_activities: List 5 devices that use heating effect
Write 5 safety rules (no wet hands, etc.)

lesson_plan.title: Fun Revision and Quiz
student_activities: Draw their favorite concept (e.g., parallel circuit)

teaching_aids: Pictures of appliances, circuit symbols chart
teacher_tip: Use lots of real-life examples and pictures. Keep explanations simple with analogies (water flow, road traffic). Focus on observation and safety awareness rather than formulas.
